message.content:
Use Playwright to test whether the 500 errors on the dashboard are fixed.

1. Go to https://nexus-ai-silk-six.vercel.app/login
2. Login with email "testuser@nexusai-test.com" and password "TestPassword123!"
3. Wait for redirect to /dashboard
4. Capture ALL network requests that return 4xx or 5xx status codes
5. Wait 5 seconds for all requests to complete
6. Take a screenshot of the dashboard
7. Save screenshot to C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\test-screenshots\08-dashboard-after-fix.png

Report whether there are still any 500 errors, and list any remaining errors found.

content.text: I'll create a Playwright test to check for 500 errors on the dashboard. Let me first set up the test file and ensure the screenshot directory exists.

input.command: ls "C:/Users/User/nexus-ai/test-screenshots" 2>/dev/null && echo "Directory exists" || echo "Directory does not exist"

cd "C:/Users/User/nexus-ai" && npx playwright --version 2>/dev/null || echo "Playwright not found"

content.text: Good, Playwright is installed. Let me create and run the test script.

input.description: Run Playwright test to check dashboard for 500 errors

content.text: The test passed. Let me verify the screenshot was saved and view it, then also check the full report.

Here is the complete report:

---

## Dashboard 500 Error Check Results

### VERDICT: No 500 errors detected. The 500 errors on the dashboard are FIXED.

### Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| **5xx errors** | **0** |
| **4xx errors** | **0** |
| **Network failures (ERR_ABORTED)** | **5** |

### Details

**Login and navigation:** The test successfully logged in with the provided credentials and was redirected to `https://nexus-ai-silk-six.vercel.app/dashboard` without any issues.

**500 errors:** Zero. No server errors were returned by any API endpoint or page route. This confirms the 500 errors have been fixed.

**4xx errors:** Zero. No client errors (401, 403, 404, etc.) were returned either.

**Network failures (non-blocking):** There were 5 `net::ERR_ABORTED` requests. These are Next.js RSC (React Server Components) prefetch requests that were cancelled by the browser, which is normal behavior during page navigation. They occur because the browser starts prefetching routes but aborts those requests when the user navigates away before they complete. These are not errors -- they are standard Next.js navigation behavior. The affected URLs:

1. `/forgot-password?_rsc=to9i9` -- prefetch aborted when navigating away from login
2. `/dashboard?_rsc=1usnx` -- RSC prefetch cancelled during transition
3. `/dashboard/surveys?_rsc=15moa` -- sidebar link prefetch cancelled
4. `/dashboard?_rsc=15moa` -- duplicate dashboard prefetch cancelled
5. `/dashboard/documents/upload?_rsc=15moa` -- sidebar link prefetch cancelled

### Screenshot

The screenshot has been saved to `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\test-screenshots\08-dashboard-after-fix.png`. It shows the dashboard loading correctly with all widgets visible:
- Four stat cards (Documents Processed, Active Surveys, Responses Collected, AI Analyses) all displaying "0"
- Recent Documents section showing "No documents yet"
- Recent Surveys section showing "No surveys yet"
- Full sidebar navigation rendering correctly (Dashboard, Documents, Upload, Templates, Surveys, Create Survey, General, Team, Billing, API Keys)
- "Documents used 3/10" quota indicator at the bottom

### Artifacts

- **Screenshot:** `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\test-screenshots\08-dashboard-after-fix.png`
- **JSON report:** `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\test-screenshots\08-dashboard-error-report.json`
- **Test file:** `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\tests\dashboard-500-check.spec.ts`
